name: Cumbres del macizo de Anboto: Alluitz y Astxiki — trailrunning por el Parque Natural de Urkiola
region_intro: Jogging around Abadiño offers diverse terrain at the foot of Urkiola Natural Park, characterized by limestone masses, steep slopes, and extensive beech forests. The region features striking karst formations and mountains like Anboto, providing challenging routes with significant elevation. Lower hills such as Montecalvo offer varied inclines and scenic views, while numerous forests and pastures create picturesque running environments. Best jogging routes around Abadiño The most popular jogging route is Cumbres del macizo de Anboto: Alluitz y Astxiki — trailrunning por el Parque Natural de Urkiola, a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the high peaks of the Anboto massif within Urkiola Natural Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Muntsaratzeko dorrea loop from Traña, a moderate 8.6 miles (13.9 km) path. This route offers a scenic loop around the Muntsaratzeko tower, providing varied terrain suitable for a steady run. Local runners also love the Urkiolaguirre Summit – Urkiola Pass loop from Abadiño, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and the Urkiola Pass, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Jogging around Abadiño is defined by limestone masses, beech forests, and challenging mountain trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult ascents. The routes in Abadiño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.4 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Abadiño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around Abadiño?
A: Jogging routes around Abadiño offer a diverse range of terrain, from the challenging limestone masses and steep slopes of &lt;strong&gt;Urkiola Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; to extensive beech forests and striking karst formations. You'll find everything from demanding mountain trails to more moderate paths through pastures and lower hills like Montecalvo, providing varied inclines and scenic views.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Abadiño?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy jogging route available. Abadiño's network includes options for different ability levels, ensuring beginners can also find suitable paths to enjoy the natural beauty.

Q: Can I find running trails with scenic viewpoints or natural landmarks in the area?
A: Absolutely! The region is rich in natural landmarks. You can find routes that pass by significant peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anboto (1,331 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3334263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urkiolaguirre Summit&lt;/a&gt;, or offer stunning vistas from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/318367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Bizkaia Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. There's also the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3009759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uguzpe Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which suggests trails with varied elevations nearby.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Abadiño?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20513135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muntsaratzeko dorrea loop from Traña&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.6-mile path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40338765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muntsaratzeko dorrea loop from Atxondo&lt;/a&gt; offers another moderate option. These loops provide varied terrain suitable for a steady run.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Abadiño?
A: The running routes in Abadiño are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.4 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the challenging yet rewarding ascents within Urkiola Natural Park.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the area around Abadiño features several beautiful bodies of water. You can find routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3147598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aixola Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5225989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albina Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7276798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendizabal Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5584355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ariola Lake Trail&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable highlight for those seeking waterside runs.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Abadiño?
A: Abadiño offers good running conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery with changing foliage. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, but higher mountain trails offer cooler air. Winter running is possible, but be prepared for potentially challenging conditions, especially in the higher parts of Urkiola Natural Park, where snow and ice can occur.

Q: Are there any challenging trail running options for experienced runners?
A: Definitely. Abadiño is an excellent destination for experienced trail runners. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058634293/cumbres-del-macizo-de-anboto-alluitz-y-astxiki-trailrunning-por-el-parque-natural-de-urkiola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cumbres del macizo de Anboto: Alluitz y Astxiki — trailrunning por el Parque Natural de Urkiola&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 7.1-mile trail, explore the high peaks of the Anboto massif with significant elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40335821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urkiolaguirre Summit – Urkiola Pass loop from Abadiño&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many running routes are available around Abadiño?
A: There are over 30 dedicated running routes around Abadiño, catering to various fitness levels and preferences. This includes a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult trails, ensuring plenty of options for every runner.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the sea or other distant landscapes?
A: Yes, some of the higher peaks and viewpoints in the region, such as Montecalvo (381 m), offer extensive views that can include the sea, the Urdaibai estuary, and the broader Duranguesado area. These routes combine varied inclines with rewarding panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical or cultural sites?
A: While the primary focus of the running routes is natural beauty, Abadiño has historical roots, including prehistoric remains in caves like Bolinkoba and Oialkoba. Some routes might pass near historical structures or offer glimpses into the region's ancient landscape, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20513135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muntsaratzeko dorrea loop from Traña&lt;/a&gt; which circles a tower.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while jogging in Urkiola Natural Park?
A: Urkiola Natural Park, with its diverse ecosystems of beech forests, pastures, and rocky outcrops, is home to a variety of wildlife. While jogging, you might spot various bird species, including raptors, and potentially encounter mammals like deer or wild boar, especially during quieter times of day. Always maintain a respectful distance and observe from afar.
tips: I loved this mountain, but it's incredibly tough on one hand, and somewhat dangerous on the other. Not so much from falling, but from slipping on the smooth rock, especially in the quarry area, on the north side.
Summit of the Urkiola Natural Park. Formerly it was called Aitz-txiki which means "small rock"
